Patch Set 2:

(1 comment)

Patch Set 2:

Writing "cleaning up *missing* objects" doesn't make sense to me. If they were missing then they wouldn't need to be cleaned up. These are precreated objects that were not used for files before the MDT rebooted.  I think just printing that this is reconnection and cleaning up unused objects then that is enough.

Patch Set 2:

> Patch Set 2:
> 
> Writing "cleaning up *missing* objects" doesn't make sense to me. If they were missing then they wouldn't need to be cleaned up.
It was my misunderstanding. 

>These are precreated objects that were not used for files before the MDT rebooted.  
Got it. Understood.

>I think just printing that this is reconnection and cleaning up unused objects then that is enough.
Ack and Thank you as always for the explaination.
Uploaded patch set 3.

commit.subject: LU-930 ofd: improve orphan cleaning message
commit.message:
LU-930 ofd: improve orphan cleaning message

Reword the orphan cleaning message, happening
at each MDT connection after OST start to make it less
scary and implies a bit more this is just normal thing
going on.

Test-Parameters: trivial

Change-Id: I7b4d726b1e96fe8d39872df5ed85453c99ccdc6a
Signed-off-by: Aurelien Degremont <adegremont@nvidia.com>
Reviewed-on: https://review.whamcloud.com/c/fs/lustre-release/+/54639
Tested-by: jenkins <devops@whamcloud.com>
Tested-by: Maloo <maloo@whamcloud.com>
Reviewed-by: Arshad Hussain <arshad.hussain@aeoncomputing.com>
Reviewed-by: Andreas Dilger <adilger@whamcloud.com>
Reviewed-by: Oleg Drokin <green@whamcloud.com>
